events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON file directly, using the scenario as a visual acceptance failure against boundary sample standards.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"medium\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.86,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Scratch/dent condition requires classification against approved boundary samples before acceptance.\",
    \"Visible surface damage is present on an installed or assembled component at rough-complete stage.\",
    \"Condition is not demonstrated to match accepted visual standard photos or PPAP-approved appearance criteria.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Segregate or tag the affected unit for quality hold.\",
    \"Compare the scratch and dent cues to the site visual standards and approved boundary samples.\",
    \"Document defect location, size, depth, and photos; disposition as rework, repair, use-as-is approval, or replacement through quality review.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The observed condition is surface appearance damage: scratches and/or dents that must be identified against acceptance-standard photos. Because the component is already installed or assembled and the visible condition has not been shown to meet the approved boundary sample, it is treated as installed-defective and non-compliant pending formal disposition.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"site visual standards\",
    \"AIAG PPAP appearance approval and boundary sample acceptance criteria\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
